originalTitle: ‘They ruined our dream’: Revenge on Socceroos’ minds for clash with Egypt
summary: Three of the current Australia squad hold painful memories of a defeat at the Tokyo Olympics that they are using for motivation in the World Cup last 32
content: The memory flashed back instantly for Socceroos midfielder Connor Metcalfe, when asked about the last time he played against Egypt. “It actually feels like a blur to be honest,” he said, recalling the Olyroos’ match five years ago at the Tokyo Olympics. “They were quite a big physical team, I just remember it being really humid and hot, we didn’t play our best game. Yeah, they ruined our dream.” The medal hopes of Metcalfe, Harry Souttar and Cammy Devlin – the only current Socceroos who played that night – ended in a disappointing 2-0 defeat when even a draw would have seen the team progress from their group. While at senior level Australia and Egypt rarely play – including most recently a 3-0 win for the Pharaohs in Cairo in 2010 – Metcalfe’s Olyroos experience has provided additional motivation. “I guess you could say it’s a bit of revenge,” he said. The speculation ahead of the match has been dominated by the fitness of Egypt forward Mo Salah, who came off against Iran with more than half an hour to play and was icing his left hamstring. The Socceroos are yet to focus their plans on Salah, however, or indeed any of the Egypt squad which also includes Manchester City player Omar Marmoush and 18-year-old forward Hamza Abdelkarim, who is signed to Barcelona B. “At the moment, we haven’t really discussed Egypt that much, we just try to refresh the body, refresh the mind, and then tomorrow onwards we’ll start to really get ready for it,” Metcalfe said. Defender Lucas Herrington said he anticipates there will be threats throughout a team that only lost narrowly to Senegal in January’s African Cup of Nations semi-final. “[Salah and Marmoush] are the two names that most people remember and most people mention, but I think we’ve just got to be ready for everyone,” he said. “They’re a top team and we’re all looking forward to the occasion and it’s another chance to make history.” The Socceroos have not won a World Cup knockout match, and both occasions they have played in the last 16 delivered painful, narrow defeats. In 2006, Italy scored a late penalty to win 1-0, and Argentina held on for a 2-1 victory in 2022. Metcalfe said they have a “really good shot” for breaking through in Dallas. “We’ve come so far, I don’t think anyone expected us to even get out of the group, so we’re all buzzing just to have this amazing achievement and be able to be in this knockout stage,” he said. Metcalfe has been instrumental for the Socceroos during this World Cup after a season where he struggled for minutes in the Bundesliga with St Pauli. He said the recent weeks have been a “release” after a frustrating period. “Just being able to let it out on the pitch and enjoy the moment and not really being nervous and just get on with it … I think it’s really helped me to be honest because there’s a lot of inner anger from the season.” The seven-day break between the Socceroos’ final group stage match and the last 32 fixture has afforded the players more time with their family in the San Francisco Bay Area, before they ramp up preparations. The squad is due to fly to Dallas on Wednesday. “It’s nice to know that we get to stay here longer,” Metcalfe said. “We also know that it could end very quickly, but we’re just trying to enjoy every day together.”
chineseTitle: “他们毁了我们的梦想”：对与埃及发生冲突的澳大利亚人的报复
chineseSummary: 澳大利亚队目前的三名队员都对东京奥运会失利怀有痛苦的回忆，他们以此为动力进入世界杯32强
chineseContent: 当被问及上次对阵埃及队的比赛时，澳大利亚队中场球员康纳·梅特卡夫的记忆立刻闪现出来。 “说实话，感觉有点模糊，”他回忆起五年前东京奥运会上奥林匹克队的比赛时说道。 “他们是一支体格庞大的球队，我只记得当时非常潮湿和炎热，我们没有打出最好的比赛。是的，他们毁了我们的梦想。”梅特卡夫、哈里·苏塔尔和卡米·德夫林（当晚唯一参加比赛的现役澳大利亚队）的奖牌希望最终以令人失望的 2-0 失利而告终，即使是平局也能让球队从小组出线。虽然澳大利亚和埃及在高级别比赛中很少比赛——包括最近 2010 年法老队在开罗 3-0 获胜的比赛——但梅特卡夫在 Olyroos 的经历提供了额外的动力。 “我想你可以说这是一种报复，”他说。赛前的猜测主要集中在埃及前锋莫·萨拉赫的健康状况上，他在对阵伊朗的比赛还剩半个多小时的时候下场，左腿筋结冰。然而，澳大利亚队还没有把他们的计划集中在萨拉赫身上，或者实际上是埃及队的任何一个球员，其中还包括曼城球员奥马尔·马穆什和与巴塞罗那 B 队签约的 18 岁前锋哈姆扎·阿卜杜勒卡里姆。“目前，我们还没有真正讨论过埃及，我们只是试图让身体恢复活力，精神焕发，然后从明天开始我们将开始真正为此做好准备，”梅特卡夫说。后卫卢卡斯·赫林顿表示，他预计这支在 1 月份的非洲国家杯半决赛中仅以微弱优势输给塞内加尔的球队将面临威胁。 “[萨拉赫和马尔穆什]是大多数人记得和大多数人提到的两个名字，但我认为我们必须为每个人做好准备，”他说。 “T
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
